events.text: Patient is a 34-year-old Caucasian female with no significant medical history and no family history of melanoma, presenting with a pigmented lesion on her right lateral neck.

At age 34, patient was diagnosed with malignant melanoma (ICD-O morphology 8720/3) of the skin of the head and neck (ICD-O topography C44.3), staged as Stage IV (T2aN1aM1d) with a synchronous solitary brain metastasis.

At age 34, excisional biopsy of the right lateral neck lesion revealed malignant melanoma with Breslow thickness of 1.5 mm, Clark level III, without ulceration, mitotic rate 2/mm2, no microsatellites, no lymphovascular invasion, and negative margins on excisional biopsy.
At age 34, wide local excision with 1-2 cm margins of the right neck melanoma was performed with primary closure.
At age 34, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the right cervical chain showed 1 of 2 sentinel nodes positive for a 1.2 mm metastatic melanoma deposit.
At age 34, sentinel lymph node pathology confirmed metastatic melanoma in 1 of 2 nodes (1.2 mm deposit), no extracapsular extension, S100 and Melan-A positive.
At age 34, staging MRI brain with gadolinium revealed a solitary 2.5 cm enhancing mass in the left occipital lobe with surrounding vasogenic edema consistent with brain metastasis.
At age 34, staging PET/CT showed FDG-avid right cervical node (post-biopsy) and no other extracranial metastatic disease.

At age 34, next-generation sequencing of the primary melanoma revealed BRAF V600E mutation (allele frequency 45%), NRAS wild-type, wild-type TP53, TERT promoter wild-type, and tumor mutational burden of 6 mutations per megabase with PD-L1 combined positive score of 8.
At age 34, serum LDH was normal at 170 U/L despite brain metastasis, and the patient had ECOG performance status of 0 with no neurological deficits aside from mild left-sided visual field changes.
At age 34, stereotactic radiosurgery was delivered to the left occipital brain metastasis at 22 Gy in 1 fraction with frameless technique.

At age 34, combination ipilimumab 3 mg/kg plus nivolumab 1 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks was initiated for 4 cycles for BRAF V600E melanoma with brain metastasis, with immunotherapy chosen first per CheckMate 204 data.
At age 34, patient developed grade 2 immune-related colitis with watery diarrhea (6 stools/day) after cycle 3, managed with oral prednisone 1 mg/kg and 1 cycle delay of therapy.
At age 34, patient developed grade 1 immune-related thyroiditis with transient thyrotoxicosis (TSH
At age 34, after completing 4 induction cycles of ipilimumab/nivolumab, nivolumab 480 mg IV every 4 weeks was continued as maintenance.
At age 34, restaging MRI brain at 12 weeks showed significant decrease in the left occipital lesion to 0.8 cm with decreased surrounding edema, consistent with treatment response.
At age 34, restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at 12 weeks showed no extracranial metastatic disease.
At age 35, LDH remained normal at 158 U/L and patient had complete resolution of visual field deficits.
At age 35, 6-month MRI brain showed continued response with the left occipital lesion now measuring 0.4 cm with peripheral enhancement likely representing treatment effect.
At age 35, 6-month PET/CT showed no extracranial metastatic disease.
At age 35, patient continued nivolumab maintenance with excellent intracranial and extracranial disease control and ECOG performance status of 0, with plan to continue for up to 2 years.
At age 35, 12-month MRI brain showed near complete resolution of the left occipital lesion with only minimal residual enhancement, and no new intracranial lesions.

At age 36, after 2 years of total immunotherapy (induction plus maintenance), nivolumab was electively discontinued given sustained complete intracranial and extracranial response with LDH normal at 149 U/L.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 34, excisional biopsy of the right lateral neck lesion revealed malignant melanoma with Breslow thickness of 1.5 mm, Clark level III, without ulceration, mitotic rate 2/mm2, no microsatellites, no lymphovascular invasion, and negative margins on excisional biopsy.

At age 34, staging MRI brain with gadolinium revealed a solitary 2.5 cm enhancing mass in the left occipital lobe with surrounding vasogenic edema consistent with brain metastasis.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 34, serum LDH was normal at 170 U/L despite brain metastasis, and the patient had ECOG performance status of 0 with no neurological deficits aside from mild left-sided visual field changes.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Stage III/IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation. Treated with combination BRAF/MEK inhibitors (dabrafenib/trametinib) and/or checkpoint immunotherapy (nivolumab plus ipilimumab, pembrolizumab). Some with brain metastases. Include LDH levels.
